**PR opened: https://github.com/pablof7z/workout.md/pull/3**

Summary of what was fixed on the runner screen:

1. **Top pill overlap** (the actual bug in your screenshot) — the floating "Circuit · 14/22" pill was colliding with the mini-map row beneath it. Root cause was a subtle SwiftUI quirk: `.overlay(alignment:)` after `.ignoresSafeArea()` still implicitly re-adds the safe-area inset, so the explicit padding was double-counting it and the reserved clearance never actually cleared the pill. Verified fixed via on-device accessibility-frame measurements (56pt clearance now, was overlapping by ~40pt before).

2. **Oversized "Rate effort" bar** — replaced with a compact two-button Liquid Glass toolbar (Rate effort + Skip/Finish, small auto-width pills, 44pt tall). Along the way I caught and fixed a real bug this introduced: the expanded effort scale's "Set" button was getting clipped by the adjacent Skip button, so I made Skip/Finish hide while the scale is expanded.

3. **Countdown generalization** — the Rest page's timer was a static, non-counting number; it's now a live auto-starting countdown ring matching the Plank-style timed hero, so every timed page behaves consistently (the timed-*set* hero was already generic per-exercise, not Plank-specific — only Rest was the gap).

All verified by building and driving the actual simulator (screenshots + accessibility frames), not just reading code. Work happened in an isolated git worktree/branch (`fix/runner-screen-polish`) to avoid stepping on the other agent working on `feat/persistence` in this repo.

**PR #3 merged into master.** The runner screen fixes (top pill overlap, compact effort/skip toolbar, live rest countdown) are live. Cleaned up the worktree and branch, and synced local `master`.

Note: master had moved forward a lot since I branched (other agents landed persistence, coach engine, and a real Plans system replacing the old `MockWorkout`) — I checked for conflicts before merging and confirmed the only overlap was one unrelated line in `RunnerView.swift`, so it merged cleanly with no risk to that other work.
from the changes that landed --- anything to apply the same changes concetpts to?

Confirmed — `RunnerView.swift` is the *only* place using this overlay pattern at all, so nothing else can have that bug. Here's the full picture:

**Nothing to apply the fixes to.** I checked all three concepts against the current codebase (which grew a lot from other merged branches — Plans, Coach, Sync, Settings, Onboarding, History):

1. **Overlay/safe-area double-count bug** — `RunnerView.swift` is the *only* file in the whole app that combines `.overlay(alignment:)` with `.ignoresSafeArea()`. No other screen uses that pattern, so no other screen can have this bug.

2. **Oversized-bar-next-to-small-button toolbar fix** — the other full-width buttons in the app (`TodayView`, `OnboardingView`, `DoneView`, `WhatsNextView`) are all single or sequential primary CTAs on their own screen/sheet — that's the correct native pattern there, not the same "two unrelated actions crammed together" problem the runner had. Nothing to change.

3. **Live-countdown pattern** — every other `seconds`/timer reference in the codebase is either a data model field, a plan editor input, or a static list-row label (`WorkoutListView` showing "45 sec" as a target, not a running clock) — all correctly static. The runner was the only place actually displaying a *live* countdown, and now both its timed pages (set + rest) do.

So this PR's fixes were scoped correctly the first time — no follow-up needed elsewhere.

Good catch to probe on — I found something real. Straight-set blocks (like Bench Press) currently have **no rest at all** between sets, not even a static one.

Digging into `Plans/PlanConversion.swift`: the flatten loop only inserts a `RestPageInfo` step for `.superset`/`.circuit` blocks, between rounds (`Plans/PlanConversion.swift:81`). The `.straight` case (line 21-40) never inserts a rest step, period — you swipe straight from Bench Press Set 1 to Set 2 to Set 3 with zero timer. This isn't a fallback to a static display like the old Rest bug — it's a full feature gap:

- `PlanEditorView.swift:135` literally hides the "Rest between rounds" stepper for `.straight` blocks (`if block.kind != .straight`), so you can't even configure it.
- `DefaultPlanSeed.swift` confirms this is by design so far — the seeded Bench Press block is created with no `restSeconds` at all, only Superset A and Circuit get one.

So real strength programs (rest 90s–3min between straight sets) currently get no timer support in this app at all — the lifter just swipes whenever they feel ready.

This is a real product gap, not a leftover instance of the bug I just fixed — but it's the natural next place to reuse the exact same countdown view we just built. Two things make me want to check with you before touching it:

1. It's a genuine feature decision (does every straight-set block get an optional rest, same as group blocks?), not just a UI fix.
2. `PlanModels.swift`/`PlanEditorView.swift`/`PlanConversion.swift` are squarely in the territory of the `feat/real-plans` work another agent just landed — there are still several other `feat/*` worktrees active on this repo, so I want to avoid colliding with in-flight work there.
